Small park from which you take the ferry to Liberty Island, where the Statue of Liberty is. The entrance, lockers and toilets (free) are in the old military battery. In the park you can also visit several memorials and the fun SeaGlass Carousel. There are many street vendors for food and souvenirs.
We visited Battery Park to cross over to Liberty Island. As you wait for the boat, you see the Statue of Liberty in the distance and the tension rises.

Park that forms the starting point for excursions to Ellis Island and the Statue of Liberty. Well organized, it allows nice views of the Upper Bay. It takes its name from the Fortress located inside the park.
